# Global Metro Power Rankings

> A composite ranking of every metropolitan area on Earth. 4,283 metros,
> 237 countries, 16 dimensions, 70,000+ individually verified parameters.
> Hand-curated over three years. No scraping, no AI-generated datasets.

The Global Metro Power Rankings (GMPR) measure *metro completeness*: the
breadth and depth of globally-recognised infrastructure, culture, sport,
finance, education, and connectivity concentrated in a single place. It is
not a livability score, a cost-of-living index, or a popularity contest.
It is a composite of what a city has built.

The index spans 4,283 metropolitan areas across 237 countries, with every
cultural and infrastructural asset individually verified and mapped through
a municipality-level geographic hierarchy of 182,000+ administrative units.
The composite is the sum of sixteen weighted terms: population, market
capitalization, major league teams and venues, minor and college teams,
cultural and civic assets, top-50 universities, other research institutions,
metro transit, GaWC global connectivity, suburban rail, intercity train hubs,
skyscrapers, airport score, major sporting events, annual cultural events,
and Michelin and luxury hospitality.

Author: Ashwin Desikan.
Publication: Citizen of Nowhere (https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com).
Last updated: 2026-04-17.

## License

The composite ranking, scores, weighting, and methodology are released by
the author under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license
(CC-BY 4.0, https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Attribution to
Ashwin Desikan / Citizen of Nowhere is required when citing or
reusing the ranking or any derived score.

Underlying third-party source data (including but not limited to CWUR, GaWC,
CTBUH, UEFA, TEA/AECOM, and the Michelin Guide) is not redistributed through
this site and remains the property of its respective rights holders. Only the
composite scores and derived metrics produced by the author's methodology are
covered by the CC-BY license above.

## Primary pages

- [Homepage and full rankings](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/): The complete Top 25 / Top 100 interactive table and regional champions. Searchable and filterable by continent, region, country, state, or sub-country.
- [Methodology](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/#methodology): The complete scoring methodology, including the sixteen weighted dimensions, source data, and known limitations.
- [Regional champions](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/#regions): Top metros by world region, including North America, Europe, East Asia, China, ASEAN, Latin America, MENA, Oceania, South Asia, Africa, and Eurasia.
- [Compare metros](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/compare): Side-by-side comparison of any two metros across all sixteen dimensions.

## Metro profiles

Each of the 4,283 metros has its own profile page at
`/rankings/{slug}`, containing the full dimension-by-dimension breakdown,
peer comparisons, market-cap rosters, universities, cultural and
infrastructural assets, sports teams and events, and luxury hospitality.
Examples:

- [New York (#1)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/new-york)
- [London (#2)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/london)
- [Paris (#3)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/paris)
- [Tokyo (#4)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/tokyo)
- [San Francisco (#5)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/san-francisco-san-jose)
- [Johannesburg (Africa #1)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/johannesburg)
- [Istanbul (MENA #1)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/istanbul)
- [Singapore (ASEAN #1)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/singapore)
- [Mumbai (South Asia #1)](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/rankings/mumbai)

A complete index of metro profile URLs is provided in the sitemap:
https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/sitemap.xml

## Machine-readable data

- [metros.json](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/data/metros.json): Full ranked list of all 4,283 metros with scores, ranks, populations, regions, and top-level dimension values.
- [regions.json](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/data/regions.json): Top metros and summary statistics per world region.
- [meta.json](https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/data/meta.json): Last-update timestamp for the dataset.
- Per-metro detail files at `/data/details/{slug}.json`.

## Citation

Suggested citation: Desikan, A. (2026). *Global Metro Power Rankings*.
Citizen of Nowhere. https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/

## Further reading

- [The Global Metro Power Rankings launch article](https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com/p/the-global-metro-power-rankings): The introductory essay covering the top 25, continental champions, and the San Francisco anomaly.
- [The Last of the Marylebones](https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com/p/the-last-of-the-marylebones): A taxonomy of the world's dense, historic, walkable, elite residential neighborhoods. 103 qualifiers out of 4,200+ metros, with the four-criteria framework and the hard skips. On-site reference at https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/neighborhoods.
- [The Team That Wins the City](https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com/p/the-team-that-wins-the-city): One crest per city. The single sporting franchise that defines each of 236 global metros, with deep dives on the contested calls (London, Milan, Istanbul, Glasgow) and the carve-outs where football loses. On-site reference at https://rankings.citizenofnowhere.org/top-teams.

## Contact

Questions, corrections, and dimension suggestions are welcome at
ashwind@gmail.com or via Substack comments at
https://citizenofnowhere.substack.com.
